text:
There was a peculiar old owl perched upon a gnarled oak tree. Every autumn, he'd weave through the trees singing a melodious hoot that would lull deer into a deep sleep.

Once, a talking squirrel asked the owl, "Why do you sing?"

With a hoot that resonated over the hills, the owl replied, "To lull the young into dreams. But don't tell them you know the whole plot!"

One autumn, a soft little brown critter ventured too close. The owl hooted gently but gave a slight nudge with his feathered foot.

The squirrel laughed. "Don't worry about them. They only listen to you."

That night, the owl spun a yarn so captivating it felt as though reality itself wove around the listener. The squirrel drifted off into sleep.

Come morning, the critter found himself back on the branch he'd nuzzled under. The owl was gone. The squirrel smiled. Perhaps there are things
